amazon_description: Import your Amazon Games library: full owned library by connecting your account, plus installed games detected automatically
amazon_login_description: Sign in to import your full Amazon Games library, including games you own but haven't installed
amazon_login_error: Failed to connect Amazon Games account.
amazon_connected_description: Your Amazon Games account is connected. You can disconnect at any time
amazon_auto_detection_description: Playlite detects installed games from the Amazon Games App automatically
amazon_windows_only_note: Available on Windows only — the Amazon Games App doesn't run reliably via Wine on Linux.
amazon_import_item_owned: Owned games, even if not installed (requires connected account)
battlenet_description: Import games installed through Battle.net (Blizzard and Activision).
battlenet_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects games installed by the Battle.net Agent.
battlenet_auto_detection_note: Detection uses the Battle.net Agent's product.db database and, when available, supplements the information with data from the aggregate.json file.
battlenet_linux_note: Battle.net does not yet run reliably through Wine. Therefore, this integration is currently available only on Windows.
battlenet_import_note: Only installed games can be detected. Battle.net does not expose your complete game library in local files.
ea_description: Import installed games and the local installation history from EA App.
ea_install_dir_description: Specify the directory where your EA App games are installed so Playlite can detect which ones are available locally. This integration is currently available only on Windows.
ea_info_note: EA does not provide a reliable way to authenticate or access your complete account library through local files.
ea_import_item_owned: Games that were previously installed, even if they are now uninstalled
ea_import_note_details: Uninstalled games are imported from EA App's local installation history and are shown as not installed.
epic_description: Import your Epic Games library: installed games through automatic detection or your complete library by connecting your account.
epic_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects Epic Games Launcher manifests to locate installed games.
epic_scanner_item_files: Detection uses .item files, which contain information such as the game name, installation directory, and executable.
epic_wine_description: On Linux, configure the Wine prefix in the Wine tab so Playlite can locate your Epic Games Launcher installation.
epic_wine_note_middle: , the manifests directory is located automatically. No additional configuration is required on this screen.
epic_import_item_owned: Owned games (purchased or claimed for free), even if they are not installed
epic_login_description: Sign in to import your complete Epic Games library, including games you own but haven't installed yet.
epic_connected_description: Your Epic Games account is connected. You can disconnect it at any time.
gog_description: Import your GOG library by connecting your account and, optionally, specify the installed games directory.
gog_connected_description: Your GOG account is connected. You can disconnect it at any time.
gog_login_description: Sign in to import your complete GOG library, including games that are not installed yet.
gog_import_item_owned: Owned games (purchased or claimed for free)
gog_import_item_installed: Installation status (when the games directory is configured)
gog_import_note_details: Only games that belong to your GOG library are imported. Games from other platforms synchronized through GOG Galaxy are not included.
gog_games_dir_description: Optional. Specify the directory where your GOG games are installed so Playlite can detect which ones are available locally. This integration is currently available only on Windows.
heroic_description: Import games installed through Heroic Games Launcher (Linux and Windows).
heroic_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects Heroic's configuration directory.
heroic_custom_dir_description: Manually specify the configuration directory if it is not detected automatically.
heroic_duplicate_warning_text: If you import games through Heroic and also through the corresponding native launcher (such as Epic Games or GOG), the same titles may appear twice in your library. This is expected: each entry represents a different platform or installation.
indiegala_description: Import games from your IndieGala account via IGClient.
indiegala_auto_detection_description: No login needed — detection is fully automatic.
indiegala_mode_description: Choose what to import from your IndieGala account.
indiegala_full_note: Games you own but never installed only get metadata (description, tags) when IGClient has cached it locally — this isn't guaranteed for every game.
legacy_description: Import games acquired via Legacy Games Launcher.
legacy_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects the state file from Legacy Games Launcher.
legacy_auto_detection_note_after: file from Legacy Games Launcher to detect acquired and installed games.
legacy_wine_description: On Linux, configure the Wine prefix in the Wine tab to detect the installation.
legacy_wine_note_before: The Wine prefix configured in the
legacy_wine_note_after: tab is automatically used to locate the launcher. No additional configuration is required on this screen.
legacy_manual_path_description: If automatic detection fails, point directly to app-state.json.
legacy_import_item_acquired: Acquired games (purchased or claimed for free)
legacy_import_note: Note: Legacy Games Launcher must be installed, and you must have logged in at least once so the state file exists.
steam_description: Import your Steam library. Import installed games and the full owned library by providing your SteamID64 and API Key.
steam_api_credentials_description: Required to import your full library and achievements.
steam_help_title: Where do I get this data?
steam_help_description: You can get your API Key for free on Steam's official site: Steam Web API. To find your SteamID64, you can use tools like STEAMID I/O.
steam_install_dir_description: Location where .acf files and executables are stored.
ubisoft_description: Import installed games and your Ubisoft Connect library.
ubisoft_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects games installed by Ubisoft Connect.
ubisoft_linux_note: On Linux, configure the Wine prefix in the Wine tab so Playlite can locate your Ubisoft Connect installation.
ubisoft_wine_description: After configuring the Wine prefix, the Ubisoft Connect directory is located automatically. No additional configuration is required on this screen.
ubisoft_import_note: Ubisoft Connect must be installed. Library games that are not installed are also imported and shown as not installed.
xbox_description: Import installed games via Gaming Services (Xbox App or Microsoft Store).
xbox_auto_detection_description: No login or folder needed — detection is fully automatic.
xbox_scanner_note: Scans every drive for a '.GamingRoot' marker file, then reads each installed game's manifest (MicrosoftGame.config). This integration is Windows-only.
xbox_import_note: Only installed games are detected. Xbox App and Microsoft Store do not offer a public endpoint to list the complete library.
xbox_gamepass_note: Games installed via Game Pass stay in your library even if they leave the Game Pass catalog or the subscription is cancelled. Only manually uninstalling the game removes it.
wine_description: Configure the Wine prefix to run Windows games on Linux.
wine_linux_only_suffix: to run games built for Windows. On native Windows, this setting has no effect. All configured platforms (Steam, Epic, Heroic, Ubisoft) use the same prefix defined here when launching games via Wine.
wine_prefix_description: Directory containing the Windows environment emulated by Wine (WINEPREFIX).
wine_how_used_description: The prefix is passed as an environment variable when starting games.
wine_command_intro: When launching a Windows game on Linux, Playlite runs:
wine_default_prefix_note: If not configured, Wine uses the default prefix
executable_save_failed: Failed to save game to the database.
executable_dialog_description: Select the main executable to manage the game.
executable_tip: Tip: Recommended files usually include the game icon and a higher internal score.
scanner_description: Find and import games installed locally outside official stores.
scanner_directory_description: Select the folder where your games are installed.
scanner_empty_state: Select a folder and click "Start Scan" to begin.
